Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (alcohol ignition interlock device programs)
Identification.ShortTitle: Criminal Code (alcohol ignition interlock device programs), An Act to amend the

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Criminal Code to allow the court to authorize repeat offenders subject to driving prohibition orders to drive, if they register in a provincial alcohol ignition interlock device program. The enactment provides that no authorization has effect until a minimum period of prohibition has been completed of 3, 6 or 12 months for a first, second or subsequent offence, respectively.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Subsection 259(1.1) of the Criminal Code is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In making the order, the court may authorize the offender to operate a motor vehicle equipped with an alcohol ignition interlock device during the prohibition period if the offender registers in an alcohol ignition interlock device program established under the law of the province in which the offender resides.
The authorization has no effect until the expiry of a period fixed by the court

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: of at least 3 months, for a first offence;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: of at least 6 months, for a second offence; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: of at least 12 months, for each subsequent offence.

Subsection.Text: The authorization applies to an offender who becomes resident in another province and registers in a program referred to in subsection (1.1) in that province.
The authorization has no effect during any period that the offender is not registered in a program referred to in subsection (1.1).
